description: Lightweight Agentic OS Work Queue route metadata. Native Hermes coordinator/reviewer calls bind aos-orchestrator per invocation without changing the sticky default; Codex and Claude remain direct workbench routes.

fallback.escalation_rule: Use Operating Hermes triage when the queue owner is missing or unknown.
fallback.policy: Fallback to the single Operating Hermes runtime through a per-invocation aos-orchestrator binding; fail instead of substituting a workbench.

orchestrator.escalation_rule: Strongest reasoning route for triage, review, and escalation judgment.
orchestrator.policy: Invoke aos-orchestrator per task for cross-lane triage, planning, review, and escalation; never switch the sticky global profile and never substitute Codex as outer coordinator.

hermes.escalation_rule: Strongest reasoning route for triage, review, and escalation judgment.
hermes.policy: Hermes is the one Operating Hermes runtime. Bind aos-orchestrator per invocation and fail clearly if that profile cannot start; do not fall back to an outer Codex coordinator.

revenue.escalation_rule: Escalate for direct prospect-facing copy, pricing/scope, unsupported claims, or after one REVISE.
revenue.policy: Cheap/fast default for routine revenue queue work; escalate only for judgment-heavy or externally visible work.

marketing.escalation_rule: Escalate for core positioning, public founder voice, offer messaging, or after one REVISE.
marketing.policy: Cheap/fast default for routine marketing production; escalate for public positioning or founder-voice decisions.

delivery.escalation_rule: Escalate for ambiguous client scope or client-facing deliverables.
delivery.policy: Mid/strong default for delivery work where correctness and client context matter.

operations.escalation_rule: Use deterministic/local first, cheap model second, and escalate rarely.
operations.policy: Prefer local deterministic handling for operational queue work before model assistance.

ops.escalation_rule: Alias of the operations lane; use deterministic/local first, cheap model second, and escalate rarely.
ops.policy: Mirror the operations route for the accepted ops owner alias.

codex.escalation_rule: Workbench metadata only; Hermes model_routes does not control Codex model selection.
codex.policy: Codex remains an implementation workbench. This metadata is stamped for receipts and ledgers only.

claude.escalation_rule: Workbench metadata only; Hermes model_routes does not control Claude Code model selection.
claude.policy: Claude Code remains an implementation workbench. This metadata is stamped for receipts and ledgers only.
